hard_skills_must_have: enterprise-scale AI and Generative AI solution design
hard_skills_nice_to_have: business intelligence and analytics platforms integrated with AI
implied_pain_points: Need an architect who can turn client business needs into scalable enterprise AI and agentic systems.
Need someone who can formalize governance, explainability, and responsible AI practices while integrating with existing enterprise platforms.
Need technical leadership that can reduce delivery risk and produce reusable cloud-native reference architectures.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show enterprise AI platform architecture, especially agentic workflows, orchestration, tool use, and production lifecycle ownership. It should also prove governance-minded delivery on real systems, not just prototypes, ideally with clear cloud and integration work.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep hands-on architecture across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and IBM Cloud if the CV does not explicitly show those platforms.
Formal ownership of AI governance, privacy, or regulatory programs without direct evidence.
Model training or fine-tuning pipeline ownership if the CV only shows inference, workflow, or platform work.
Client consulting delivery at enterprise scale if that context is not explicitly documented.
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps well because it is an enterprise AI workflow platform with 42 plugins, 2,000 users, RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, and guardrails.
Lantern maps well because multi-provider routing and circuit breaking show production AI platform thinking and reliability design.
Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group maps well because it demonstrates enterprise context and technical leadership across AI platform work.

hard_skills_must_have: AI system architecture design and implementation
implied_pain_points: Need to scale an FSI-focused AI consultancy while keeping technical delivery strong.
Need an architect who can also lead AI engineers, improve performance, and communicate clearly with clients.
Need safer, compliant multi-agent AI systems that can be delivered consistently for financial services work.

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 7+ years hands-on AI/ML solutions development; 3+ years technical leadership in AI project delivery; 2+ years team management and performance development

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show that the candidate has led AI delivery, not just built features: team leadership, client communication, and performance improvement should be visible. It should also show real architecture ownership for multi-agent or production AI systems with safety and compliance awareness.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Direct people management, performance reviews, or PIP ownership if the CV does not explicitly show it.
BFSI or financial-services client delivery experience if it is not explicitly documented.
Business development, upsell, or account-expansion ownership without clear evidence.
Formal consultancy leadership across multiple teams if the CV only shows technical lead scope.
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the best fit because it proves enterprise AI platform delivery, evaluation, and guardrails in a real production setting.
Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group is relevant because it shows senior technical leadership in an enterprise environment.
Lantern supports the architecture side because it demonstrates production AI gateway design, routing, and resilience.

hard_skills_must_have: AWS Certified Solutions Architect Professional or AWS Certified Machine Learning - Specialty
implied_pain_points: Need someone who can keep cloud operations running while also shipping AI/ML solutions for BFSI clients.
Need to coordinate hiring, capacity planning, and partner-program work without losing technical depth.
Need reusable cloud and AI delivery patterns that reduce cost and accelerate project delivery.

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 5+ years cloud architecture, solution architecture, or cloud engineering; 2+ years working with AI/ML workloads on cloud platforms

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should prove both sides of the role: hands-on cloud/AI delivery and operational ownership. The best evidence shows production Python services, IaC, containerization, and examples of reducing cost or improving delivery processes.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Ownership of AWS funding applications, AWS/GCP partner programs, or Well-Architected review delivery if not explicitly shown.
Direct experience with AWS Bedrock, ECS, EKS, or Terraform in production if the CV does not name them.
Formal hiring-pipeline coordination or cloud department operational leadership if not explicitly demonstrated.
AWS certification claims if the candidate does not actually hold the required certification.
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lantern is a strong match because multi-provider routing and circuit breaking map to reliable production AI platform thinking.
Commander-4 is relevant because it shows an enterprise AI workflow platform with governance, evaluation, and operationalized delivery.
Lead Engineer experience in CQRS/event-sourcing and the microservices/RabbitMQ background map to architecture, distributed systems, and delivery discipline.
Backend Engineer work with Flask/MongoDB maps to the Python backend implementation expected in this role.

architecture.latency_reliability: performance, resilience, security, latency and cost considerations, SLOs

success_metrics: clear boundaries between product teams and shared services
implied_pain_points: Codify reference architectures and patterns so future squads can deliver consistently.
Define security, governance, ALM, and integration standards for AI agent delivery.
Reduce complexity while keeping the platform cost-efficient and resilient.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV must show that the candidate has designed and governed real AI platforms, not just prototypes. It should evidence RAG, agent orchestration, ALM/CI/CD, security/DLP, telemetry, and cost-aware delivery at production scale.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep Microsoft Power Platform or Copilot Studio ownership at enterprise scale unless explicitly evidenced
Formal hiring or performance management responsibility
Budget or P&L ownership
Hands-on Purview/DLP administration unless clearly documented
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps directly to enterprise AI workflow architecture, RAG, guardrails, evaluation, and user-scale delivery.
Lantern maps to multi-provider routing, resilience patterns, and cost-aware platform design.
Technical Lead experience at Seven.One supports reference architecture, coaching, and cross-squad delivery.

architecture.latency_reliability: performance, resilience, security, latency and cost considerations, SLOs

success_metrics: clear boundaries between product teams and shared services
implied_pain_points: Codify reference architectures and patterns so future squads can deliver consistently.
Define security, governance, ALM, and integration standards for AI agent delivery.
Reduce complexity while keeping the platform cost-efficient and resilient.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV must show that the candidate has designed and governed real AI platforms, not just prototypes. It should evidence RAG, agent orchestration, ALM/CI/CD, security/DLP, telemetry, and cost-aware delivery at production scale.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep Microsoft Power Platform or Copilot Studio ownership at enterprise scale unless explicitly evidenced
Formal hiring or performance management responsibility
Budget or P&L ownership
Hands-on Purview/DLP administration unless clearly documented
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps directly to enterprise AI workflow architecture, RAG, guardrails, evaluation, and user-scale delivery.
Lantern maps to multi-provider routing, resilience patterns, and cost-aware platform design.
Technical Lead experience at Seven.One supports reference architecture, coaching, and cross-squad delivery.

architecture.latency_reliability: high-performance APIs, observability, reliability, and performance at platform scale

success_metrics: design and implement scalable microservices architectures
ensure observability, reliability, and performance at platform scale
implied_pain_points: Turn platform architecture into working production code instead of just designs.
Scale microservices and APIs for AI and simulation workflows.
Add observability, reliability, and performance discipline to a hands-on platform team.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV must prove hands-on platform delivery in production: building microservices, operating Kubernetes, and shipping AI/ML pipelines that run outside notebooks. The best evidence is end-to-end ownership of APIs, observability, and reliability, plus any industrial or simulation-heavy work.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Strong industrial domain delivery in automotive, construction, railway, naval, or aerospace if not actually shipped there
Production Golang leadership if Go was not a primary production language
Kubernetes operations at scale if experience was limited or non-production
ML/RL deployment in production if work stayed at notebook or prototype stage
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lead Engineer work on CQRS and event sourcing maps to distributed systems and event-driven architecture.
Microservices and RabbitMQ experience maps to the required microservices and orchestration stack.
Commander-4 and Lantern show production platform ownership, resilience patterns, and AI platform operations.
WebRTC and other real-time systems map to performance-sensitive production engineering, though the industrial domain itself is still a gap.

architecture.scale_signals: large-scale datasets from X-ray, CT, MRI, and digital pathology

hard_skills_nice_to_have: FDA/CE Class B or C medical imaging products
implied_pain_points: Need to design and optimize healthcare computer vision solutions for medical image analysis.
Need to make models production-ready with robust validation, explainability, and regulatory readiness.
Need to support presales and client communication across healthcare use cases.

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 7+ years in AI/ML with at least 4 years focused on computer vision and deep learning

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show enterprise AI platform architecture, production delivery, and disciplined model governance. It should also show some credible computer vision or medical-imaging adjacency, because the role is explicitly healthcare CV and the JD asks for CNNs, GANs, DICOM/NIfTI, and PACS integration.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep medical imaging domain expertise
hands-on CNN or GAN research specialization
DICOM or NIfTI pipeline ownership
PACS integration leadership
FDA or CE regulated imaging product ownership
published computer vision research
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps well to the enterprise AI workflow, evaluation, and guardrails parts of the role, but not to the medical imaging domain itself.
Lantern maps to platform reliability and multi-provider integration patterns that can support production AI systems.
Technical Lead experience at Seven.One maps to cross-functional delivery and communicating technical tradeoffs to non-technical stakeholders.

architecture.scale_signals: large-scale, complex AI / Data / Cloud platform programs involving multiple cross-functional teams and external vendors
architecture.latency_reliability: scalability, security, and performance across the organization

success_metrics: technical consensus on major design decisions
implied_pain_points: Need one accountable leader to coordinate multiple workstreams and external vendors on a complex AI/data/cloud program.
Need to make architecture decisions across cloud topology, deployment model, and platform components.
Need to keep delivery predictable in a multi-vendor environment while handling risk and stakeholder alignment.

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 10+ years of professional experience in software engineering with at least 5 years in an architecture-focused role

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to prove senior architecture ownership across large systems, plus enough delivery leadership to show the candidate can coordinate cross-functional teams and make hard tradeoffs. The JD also wants governance and execution, so evidence of reliable platform delivery matters as much as architecture depth.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: formal engineering manager scope with direct reports
budget or P&L ownership
multi-vendor program governance at enterprise scale
air-gapped Kubernetes program ownership
deep Azure or GCP enterprise architecture leadership
executive-level stakeholder control over a large AI program
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps to platform architecture, AI workflow delivery, and governance patterns such as evaluation and guardrails.
Lantern maps to reliable multi-provider routing, circuit breaking, and platform control-plane design.
Lead Engineer work on CQRS/event sourcing and microservices/RabbitMQ maps to distributed-system design and architecture tradeoffs.

architecture.scale_signals: enterprise systems and agentic platforms for business and IT operations
architecture.latency_reliability: resilient, observable, secure, and production-ready; optimize for cost, scale, and performance

implied_pain_points: Need an architect who can define secure patterns for agentic and automation workflows without requiring deep AI specialization from day one.
Need to guide customers from simple automation toward more advanced agentic ecosystems.
Need governance, observability, and enterprise readiness around new AI-powered workflows.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show hands-on architecture for production systems, plus enough AI-platform experience to credibly discuss agentic workflows, guardrails, and operational readiness. It should also show customer-facing solution design, because the JD explicitly includes discovery, proposals, SoWs, and PoCs.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep Java or TypeScript implementation leadership if not already demonstrated
formal ownership of security and compliance programs
expert-level agentic AI research credentials
customer-presales ownership at enterprise scale
full-stack cloud-native architecture mastery across all listed stacks
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest fit because it already combines RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, and guardrails in an enterprise AI workflow platform.
Lantern maps to multi-provider routing, circuit breaking, and scalable control-plane patterns that fit the role's focus on resilient enterprise AI systems.
Technical Lead experience maps to the hands-on architecture, stakeholder communication, and customer-facing collaboration expected in discovery and PoCs.

success_metrics: translate complex requirements into actionable roadmaps
implied_pain_points: need for a sovereign AI architecture that meets national regulatory requirements
need to build secure and performant AI platforms
need to translate complex customer and executive requirements into executable roadmaps
need to balance innovation with governance, privacy, and compliance
need to guide organizations through AI infrastructure complexity

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 10+ years in enterprise IT, cloud, AI/ML, or data/analytics

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show you have designed and owned enterprise AI platforms, not just used models. It should prove experience with governance, security/privacy, and stakeholder-facing architecture decisions, ideally in environments where reliability and compliance mattered.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Owned a national sovereign AI compliance program end to end without direct evidence
Delivered FedRAMP-governed AI deployments without explicit proof
Led enterprise-wide AI governance for regulated customers unless documented
Managed hiring or performance management for a large org unless the CV shows it
Claimed deep GreenLake production experience unless it is explicitly evidenced
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps most directly because it demonstrates enterprise AI workflow platform design, RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, guardrails, and adoption at 2000-user scale.
Lantern maps well because it shows LLM gateway design, multi-provider routing, and resilience patterns like circuit breakers, which are relevant to secure AI platform architecture.
Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group supports the architecture-leadership and stakeholder-advisory aspects of the role.
Prior CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices experience strengthens the case for distributed architecture and end-to-end platform design.
